---
title: "Exploring Birds Data"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-25"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

## Load the libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(naniar)
```
## Data
```{r}
bird_collision <- read.csv("data/bird_collision.csv")  %>% clean_names()
```

##Variables collected: 
  date (mm/dd/yyyy)
  year (yyyy)
  yday (year day (Julian day))
  building (unique ID (anonymous))
  volunteer (unique ID (anonymous))
  count (# birds found)
  uwind (East-West component of wind (zonal wind))
    + values: wind is blowing from east to west
    - values: wind is blowing from west to east
  vwind (North-South component of wind(Meridional wind))
    + values: wind is blowing from south to north 
    - values: wind is blowing from the north toward the south
  ceiling height (m): average nightly height of the lowest cloud layer that covers at least 5/8 of the sky 
  visibility distance (m): average horizontal distance at which object can be identified
  Temperature (C): nightly average temperature recorded at ground level
  AMT (average migration traffic): nightly average number of birds flying through a specific area of airspace(measured through weather radar)...birds/km/hr.. volume

```{r}
glimpse(bird_collision)
```
##any NA? 
```{r}
miss_var_summary(bird_collision) #doesn't seem like there's any na.. which lwk makes sense given the types data collected
```
##how many years are there in the data set? 
```{r}
bird_collision %>% 
  distinct(year) #there's 2017 to 2020 
```

##is there a similar monthly trend every year in bird collision count ? 
```{r}
bird_collision %>% 
  separate(col="date", into=c("month", "day", "year"), sep = "/") %>% 
  select(year, month, count) %>% 
  mutate(month=as.factor(month)) %>% 
  group_by(year, month) %>% 
  summarize(monthly_collision = sum(count), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = month, y = monthly_collision)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = month)) +
  facet_grid(~year) + 
  labs ( title = "Monthly Bird Collisions by year", x = "Month", y = "Total Collisions")
```

##does having a east wind (east to west) result in different # of collisions than west wind (west to east)
```{r}
bird_collision %>% 
  select(uwind, count) %>% 
  mutate(wind_dir = ifelse(uwind > 0, "east", "west"),
         wind_dir = as.factor(wind_dir)) %>% 
  group_by(wind_dir) %>% 
  summarize(total_collision = sum(count), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = wind_dir, y = total_collision)) + 
  geom_col(aes(fill = wind_dir)) + 
  labs (title = "Wind Direction and Collision count", x = "Wind Direction", y = "Total Collision", fill = "Wind Direction")
  #could be interesting to look by year too lwk 
  #lwk... bird collision is never a continuous so no scatter plot?? I guess could look at uwind and visibility but not sure if there are relevant continuous data... 
```

```{r}
bird_collision %>% 
  select(uwind, count) %>% 
  mutate(wind_dir = ifelse(uwind > 0, "east", "west"),
         wind_dir = as.factor(wind_dir)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = wind_dir, y = count)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = wind_dir))+
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Daily Bird Collisions by Wind Direction", x = "Wind Direction", y = "Daily Collision Count") 
```

##visibility vs. collision 
```{r}
#could also do boxplot, honestly idk which is better
bird_collision %>% 
  select(visibility, count) %>% 
  mutate(visibility = as.factor(visibility)) %>% 
  group_by(visibility) %>% 
  summarize(total_collision = sum(count)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = visibility, y = total_collision)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = visibility)) +
  labs(title = "Visibility vs. Collision", x = "Visibility", y = "Collision")
```
